name: JAX Distributed On Slurm
summary: Plan and smoke test distributed JAX jobs on Slurm GPU allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for checking JAX imports, accelerator visibility, multi-process initialization, process metadata, and a small compiled computation before launching real distributed JAX workloads.

inputs.name: gpu_partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue that provides GPU nodes.

inputs.name: jax_environment
inputs.description: Module, virtual environment, Conda environment, or container that provides jax and jaxlib for the target accelerator backend.

inputs.name: resources
inputs.description: Slurm nodes, tasks per node, GPUs per node, CPUs, memory, and wall time requested for the JAX smoke test.

inputs.name: coordinator_port
inputs.description: TCP port used by process 0 for JAX distributed initialization.

outputs.name: jax_smoke_log
outputs.description: Slurm log containing JAX version, backend, device visibility, process metadata, and smoke-test status.

outputs.name: launch_record
outputs.description: Reviewable launch plan with selected coordinator address, process count, and dry-run or execution mode.

examples.title: Plan or run a distributed JAX smoke test

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Check the Python smoke script syntax without requiring JAX to be installed.

Replace account, partition, and environment placeholders, submit with RUN_JAX_SMOKE=0 to review the launch plan, then submit with RUN_JAX_SMOKE=1 on a short GPU allocation and confirm every process reports a JAX backend and status=ok.
